title: aquilegia formosa var. formosa
intro: Aquilegia formosa var. formosa, also known as the Western columbine, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may be attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Aquilegia formosa var. formosa include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making a tea from its flowers and leaves, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
